Description: An infection or inflammation of the middle ear  often causing pain fever  and hearing difficulties  commonly seen in children.
An infection of the outer ear canal  often due to water exposure leading to itching ,pain and discharge.
Inflammation or infection of the sinuses  causing nasal congestion  facial pain  and thick nasal discharge.
An allergic reaction affecting the nasal passages  triggered by allergens like pollen  causing sneezing  runny nose  and itchy eyes.
Inflammation of the tonsils  usually due to bacterial or viral infection leading to sore throat and difficulty swallowing.
Inflammation of the larynx often causing hoarseness or loss of voice typically due to viral infection or vocal strain.
A disorder of the inner ear causing vertigo tinnitus hearing loss and a feeling of fullness in the ear.
A condition where the nasal septum is misaligned leading to breathing difficulties and recurrent sinus infections.
Perception of noise or ringing in the ears without an external source often linked to hearing loss or ear injury.
Nosebleeds caused by irritation  or dry air affecting the nasal mucosa often recurring in dry climates.
